stories.title: West takeaways: Rockets survive Game 4 to keep season alive
stories.summary: Here's what has stood out from the latest playoff games of the Western Conference's first round.

stories.title: Rockets stay alive, find belief in Sengun's speech
stories.summary: The Rockets staved off playoff elimination with a 115-96 win over the Lakers in Game 4 on Sunday night and credited Alperen Sengun's speech from morning shootaround for helping them believe they could come back in the series.

stories.title: Wemby back, unhappy with concussion process
stories.summary: Victor Wembanyama had 27 points, 12 rebounds and seven blocks in his return from a concussion as the Spurs beat the Blazers for a 3-1 series lead.

stories.title: LeBron says loss 'started with me,' cites turnovers
stories.summary: LeBron James shouldered the blame for the Lakers' 115-96 loss in Game 4 of their first-round Western Conference series against the Rockets on Sunday after he racked up nearly as many turnovers (eight), as he did points (10) in the blowout.

stories.title: Tomlin: Exit partly rooted in recent playoff woes
stories.summary: Mike Tomlin told NBC's Maria Taylor that he walked away, in part, because of the recent lack of playoff success and a belief that some of his veterans were "worthy of the excitement and the optimism associated with new leadership."
